mission: Help one million people enjoy and protect the ocean. Build an audience and commerce company around ocean stewardship — not an ocean conservation company first. Conservation projects become the mission; educational content becomes the traffic; products become the cash flow. Every piece of content should teach, inspire, or help someone choose equipment, building trust that drives sustainable revenue across planet (education, cleanup resources, conservation partnerships), people (helping beginners discover diving, snorkeling, beaches, and sustainable travel), and profit (affiliate sales, travel partnerships, memberships, branded products, software tools, and eventually proprietary equipment).

high-level-strategy.what-drives-traffic: Educational SEO content is the primary traffic engine — best-of guides, equipment reviews, beach and destination guides, marine species profiles, diving guides, cleanup organization directories, and ocean facts published at scale (targeting thousands of pages over time). Distribution runs through Google SEO, Pinterest, YouTube, and Instagram. Technology differentiators — interactive beach maps, ocean cleanup maps, AI equipment advisors, dive logbooks, marine life trackers, and reef explorers — create return visits and moats that WordPress-only competitors cannot match. A weekly newsletter and growing community multiply reach. The Ocean Explorer Ecosystem (cleanup, diving, snorkeling, beaches, marine wildlife, sustainable travel, ocean technology) keeps content cohesive under one umbrella rather than scattering across unrelated niches.
high-level-strategy.what-generates-revenue: Revenue follows a phased distribution strategy — not a rush to own inventory or OEM deals on day one. Phase 1 (0–12 months): affiliate commissions only via trusted middlemen (Amazon, Diveinn, REI, dive retailers) who already solve logistics and convert; your real product is attention + trust + buying intent. Phase 2 (12–24 months): grow email list and audience, then contact brands with referral data to negotiate better affiliate rates — not cold partnership emails. Phase 3 (24–48 months): selective OEM direct deals on proven top sellers; keep affiliate fallback for everything else. Phase 4 (48+ months): own-brand products (highest margin) backed by OEM supply chain for scale. Long-term tiers: travel affiliates, membership, Web3 loyalty, software licensing, and proprietary equipment manufacturing.

distribution-strategy.core-principle: OEM relationships are earned through distribution power, not granted via outreach. Your real product early on is attention + trust + buying intent in the ocean niche — not dive gear, snorkels, or ocean tech.
distribution-strategy.middlemen-are-useful: Do not avoid middlemen early. Amazon, Diveinn/scuba retailers, REI, and local dive shops already solved logistics, convert well, and provide instant monetization while you fund and validate your audience.
good-oem-relations-means: Sending brands sales they didn't have before
Being a consistent, high-quality referring channel
Publishing product education that improves their conversion rate
good-oem-relations-is-not: Pushing for direct deals before you have referral volume to show

phase-1.focus: Build content, SEO, tools, and audience; track what converts

phase-2.monetization: Affiliate + improved rates from proven referral data
phase-2.focus: Email list growth; contact brands you already send sales to; negotiate better commissions

phase-3.monetization: Select OEM direct deals on top sellers + affiliate fallback for all else
phase-3.focus: Direct referral partnerships with brands that already benefit from your traffic

phase-4.monetization: Own-brand products (highest margin) + OEM supply chain for scaling
phase-4.focus: Launch proprietary products; use OEM manufacturing relationships earned in Phase 3

affiliate-channels: Leisure Pro / House of Scuba / similar dive e-commerce

four-pillars.sales: Become the trusted recommendation site for ocean exploration and stewardship — snorkel, cleanup, boating, scuba, and ocean technology through educational content that converts.
four-pillars.technology: Leverage Rust, Web3, AI, and React to build interactive maps, cleanup trackers, dive logbooks, AI equipment advisors, and NFT memberships that competitors on WordPress cannot replicate.
four-pillars.community: Network with dive instructors, marine biologists, ocean photographers, cleanup organizers, conservation NGOs, YouTubers, and reef scientists — build tools that help them, not just extract value.
four-pillars.education: AI-assisted content production at 30–45 min/day (~5 hrs/week): articles on beaches, marine species, diving guides, equipment reviews, and destination guides.

brand-positioning.north-star-question: How do I help someone fall in love with the ocean?
brand-positioning.principle: Start with products that build credibility, not products that make the most money. People who fall in love with the ocean eventually buy snorkel gear, scuba equipment, cameras, electric boats, cleanup gear, and conservation experiences — because they care, not because they were sold to.

pillars.explore: Snorkel, scuba, marine travel, beaches, ocean life identification
pillars.protect: Cleanup gear, conservation education, volunteer events, reef etiquette
pillars.innovate: Electric boats, solar marine systems, ocean technology, desalination
pillars.restore: Reef projects, mangroves, citizen science, NGO partnerships

content-layers.layer-1-traffic: People arrive searching: best snorkel, reef-safe sunscreen, beginner destinations
content-layers.layer-2-education: They stay discovering: coral reefs, ocean science, cleanup events, marine biology
content-layers.layer-3-mission: They become: cleanup community, volunteers, ocean advocates, sustainable purchasers

product-line-priority.why: High-ticket, research-heavy niche — perfect for affiliate after credibility established

product-line-priority.label: Water Purification (Ocean Technology)

product-line-priority.label: Ocean Tech & Innovation
product-line-priority.why: Unique differentiator — ocean drones, AI reef monitoring, robotics, autonomous cleanup vessels

domain-setup-checklist.registration: Register for 10 years (or max allowed); optional .com redirect later if cheap
steps: Strong unique password stored in a password manager
Enable 2FA on the registrar account (authenticator app preferred over SMS)
Enable registrar lock on the domain to block unauthorized transfers
Turn on auto-renew so the domain never lapses
Use a permanent admin email for WHOIS/account (not a throwaway)

ten-year-vision.hub: ocean-love.xyz — single site for content, commerce, community, and conservation

shop.role: Trusted commerce hub — Phase 1–2: middleman affiliate links; Phase 3: OEM direct deals + affiliate fallback; Phase 4: own-brand + OEM supply chain

evolution: ocean-love.xyz/shop — commerce hub rolled out by credibility order: snorkel → cleanup → boating → scuba → ocean tech
ocean-love.xyz/explore — destinations, beaches, marine life, dive guides, interactive maps
ocean-love.xyz/cleanup — cleanup org directory, events, volunteer tools, NGO partnerships
ocean-love.xyz/marketplace — vetted eco-travel, dive trips, resorts, liveaboards, partner listings
ocean-love.xyz/community — newsletter, membership, Web3 loyalty, largest eco-diving community

estimated_hours_assumptions.content_philosophy: Quality over quantity — fewer pillar articles, fully researched and validated; product catalog depth kept; ship what fits in ~100 hrs and roll the rest forward
estimated_hours_assumptions.workflow: Cursor AI for research, drafting, and site/tool builds; founder validates facts, edits voice, publishes, and distributes
estimated_hours_assumptions.per_quality_article: ~3 hrs (research + AI draft + validate/edit + publish + social)
estimated_hours_assumptions.per_product_entry: ~20 min (AI research + validate + product.json + affiliate link)
estimated_hours_assumptions.per_youtube_video: ~3–4 hrs (script via AI, record/edit, publish + link back)
estimated_hours_assumptions.technology_with_cursor: ~3–5× faster than solo; MVPs still need validation and integration time
estimated_hours_assumptions.pace_reference: ~100 hrs per block; rough estimate — roll forward if over budget
